edges.description: Endothelial injury promotes intravascular thrombosis and fibrointimal arteriopathy.
Cutaneous microvascular occlusion produces the characteristic atrophic papules.
Mesenteric involvement can cause abdominal pain and bowel symptoms.
Gastrointestinal microvascular occlusion can progress to ischemia and perforation.
Central nervous system infarction can occur from small-vessel occlusion.
Serosal and pericardial involvement can culminate in constrictive pericarditis.
Persistent vascular injury can extend into a fibrosing reaction in the surrounding tissues.

nodes.description: Abdominal pain often reflects gastrointestinal tract involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Malignant atrophic papulosis treated with eculizumab and hirudin: a fatal case report and literature review.

nodes.description: Complement activation and enhanced type I interferon signaling damage vascular endothelial cells and initiate the vasculopathic process.

evidence.reference_title: Exploring the pathophysiologic basis of constrictive pericarditis of Kohlmeier Degos disease: A case series and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: There was significant upregulation in type I interferon signaling in cases tested as revealed by the degree of staining for MXA, the surrogate type I interferon marker.

evidence.reference_title: The Evolution in the Cutaneous and Gastrointestinal Pathology of Pediatric Kohlmeier-Degos Disease Under Combined Modality Therapy.
evidence.snippet: complement pathway activation and enhanced type I interferon signaling are held to be a critical impetus for the endothelial cell injury

nodes.description: Serosal and pericardial fibrosis can lead to constrictive pericarditis and restrictive cardiopulmonary failure.

evidence.reference_title: Exploring the pathophysiologic basis of constrictive pericarditis of Kohlmeier Degos disease: A case series and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: A potentially lethal complication of Kohlmeier-Degos disease is constrictive pericarditis and pleuritis

nodes.description: A fibrosing reaction develops in affected tissues, contributing to serosal and pericardial restriction.

evidence.reference_title: Exploring the pathophysiologic basis of constrictive pericarditis of Kohlmeier Degos disease: A case series and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: A potentially lethal complication of Kohlmeier-Degos disease is constrictive pericarditis and pleuritis

evidence.reference_title: The Evolution in the Cutaneous and Gastrointestinal Pathology of Pediatric Kohlmeier-Degos Disease Under Combined Modality Therapy.
evidence.snippet: Extravascular fibrosis with a loss in the expression of CD34 amid fibroblasts was observed

nodes.description: Mesenteric and intestinal vascular occlusion can produce bowel ischemia and perforation.

evidence.reference_title: The Evolution in the Cutaneous and Gastrointestinal Pathology of Pediatric Kohlmeier-Degos Disease Under Combined Modality Therapy.
evidence.snippet: evidence of acute severe intestinal ischemia

evidence.reference_title: Malignant atrophic papulosis treated with eculizumab and hirudin: a fatal case report and literature review.

nodes.description: Characteristic porcelain-white atrophic papules with erythematous or telangiectatic rims are the defining skin lesion.

evidence.snippet: The pathognomonic skin lesions evolve over time and are large papules with an atrophic porcelain-white center and an erythematous rim

evidence.reference_title: The Evolution in the Cutaneous and Gastrointestinal Pathology of Pediatric Kohlmeier-Degos Disease Under Combined Modality Therapy.

nodes.description: Cerebral small-vessel occlusion can lead to infarction and stroke.

evidence.snippet: Gastrointestinal involvement and central nervous system infarctions are the most frequent causes of death

nodes.description: The disease evolves into a thrombotic microvascular and arteriopathic vasculopathy with microangiopathy, neointimal hyperplasia, and obliterative vascular occlusion.

evidence.reference_title: Exploring the pathophysiologic basis of constrictive pericarditis of Kohlmeier Degos disease: A case series and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: a thrombogenic microangiopathy to a fibrointimal obliterative arteriopathy with an accompanying background of extravascular fibrosis

evidence.reference_title: The Evolution in the Cutaneous and Gastrointestinal Pathology of Pediatric Kohlmeier-Degos Disease Under Combined Modality Therapy.
evidence.snippet: endothelial cell necrosis and microvascular thrombosis and arteriolar and arterial neointimal hyperplasia with intimal fibrosis
